title: micradenia riedelii
intro: Micradenia riedelii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the Riedel's micradenia or wild orange. It is native to Queensland, Australia, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, providing several health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal relief. Micradenia riedelii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
